message.content: Recall FrameLog project memory, read CLAUDE.md, then run ./gradlew compileDebugKotlin to verify clean build. Summarize current project status before proceeding.

1→---
     2→name: FrameLog implementation status
     3→description: Full implementation status of the FRAME//LOG Android app — data, VM, and UI layers
     4→type: project
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Fully complete layers (compiling cleanly as of 2026-03-19)
     8→
     9→**Database / data layer:**
    10→- All 12 Room entities in `data/db/entity/`
    11→- All enums in `Enums.kt`: CameraBodyFormat, FilmFormat, ShutterIncrements, ApertureIncrements, ColorType, RollStatus
    12→- TypeConverters for all enums (string values, not ordinals)
    13→- All 11 DAOs in `data/db/dao/`
    14→- Relation types in `data/db/relation/`: RollWithDetails, FrameWithDetails, KitWithDetails, + join relations
    15→- Export types in `RollExport.kt`
    16→- AppDatabase: singleton, all DAO accessors, 3 multi-DAO @Transaction methods
    17→- All 4 repositories in `data/repository/`
    18→
    19→**Infrastructure:**
    20→- `data/AppPreferences.kt` — SharedPreferences wrapper (activeRollId, onboarding, settings, per-roll currentFrame + MRU filters)
    21→- `ui/util/ExposureValues.kt` — shutter speed + aperture value lists, display formatters, EC values
    22→- `ui/util/ExportFormatter.kt` — CSV, JSON, plain text formatters for RollExport
    23→
    24→**ViewModel layer (13 VMs):**
    25→GearLibraryViewModel, LensDetailViewModel, CameraBodyDetailViewModel, FilterDetailViewModel,
    26→FilmStockDetailViewModel, KitDetailViewModel, KitSelectorViewModel, RollListViewModel,
    27→RollSetupViewModel, RollJournalViewModel, FrameDetailViewModel, QuickScreenViewModel, SettingsViewModel
    28→
    29→---
    30→
    31→## UI layer — Gear Library screens complete (verified compiling as of 2026-03-20)
    32→
    33→**Done and compiling clean:**
    34→- `ui/navigation/NavRoutes.kt` — all type-safe @Serializable route definitions (Navigation Compose 2.8.0)
    35→- `ui/navigation/NavGraph.kt` — FrameLogNavGraph NavHost; all Gear Library routes wired, Roll/Quick/Settings routes commented as TODO
    36→- `MainActivity.kt` — updated to use FrameLogNavGraph + rememberNavController
    37→- `ui/gear/GearLibraryScreen.kt` — full 5-tab implementation (Lenses, Bodies, Filters, Film, Kits); ScrollableTabRow, search + sort dropdown, LazyColumn, gear cards for all types, EmptyState; defines ColorType.label (internal)
    38→- `ui/gear/LensDetailScreen.kt` — full form; defines shared internal composables **MountTypeField** and **EnumDropdown**, plus ApertureIncrements.label
    39→- `ui/gear/CameraBodyDetailScreen.kt` — full form; defines CameraBodyFormat.label and ShutterIncrements.label

    80→  - Simple searchable kit list with kit cards (name, body, primary lens + count, filters)
    81→  - KitSelected event writes kit.kit.id to previousBackStackEntry?.savedStateHandle["selected_kit_id"], then pops back
    82→  - FAB navigates to KitDetail(0) for new kit creation
    83→  - NavGraph: KitSelector route wired
    84→  - **Bug fixed (crash):** original shared-ViewModel approach (getBackStackEntry) crashed; replaced with SavedStateHandle result-passing pattern
    85→  - **Bug fixed (silent failure):** RollSetupViewModel.observeKitSelection() observed the ViewModel's own SavedStateHandle, which is a DIFFERENT object from NavBackStackEntry.savedStateHandle (the navigation-results handle that KitSelectorScreen writes to). Observation moved to RollSetupScreen composable via LaunchedEffect(navController) → navController.currentBackStackEntry?.savedStateHandle?.getStateFlow("selected_kit_id", -1). ViewModel now exposes loadAndApplyKit(kitId: Int) instead of observing internally.
    86→
    87→**Icon set note:** Project uses material-icons-core only (no extended). Confirmed safe core icons: ArrowBack (AutoMirrored.Filled), KeyboardArrowRight (AutoMirrored.Filled), Search, Clear, Check, Add, MoreVert, KeyboardArrowDown, KeyboardArrowUp. ExpandMore/ExpandLess, LocationOn, Mic are NOT in core. Substitutions: KeyboardArrowDown/Up for expand/collapse; OutlinedButton("Mic") text button for speech; text link for GPS coordinates.
    88→
    89→- `ui/frames/FrameDetailScreen.kt` — complete and compiling (2026-03-20)
    90→  - TopAppBar: back arrow (unsaved-changes-aware), "Frame N" title, logged timestamp + "GPS logged" subtitle
    91→  - Logged toggle: FilterChip (yes/no)
    92→  - Lens picker row: tappable → ModalBottomSheet with LensPickerItem list ("current" trailing label)
    93→  - Filter chips: FlowRow, MRU-ordered, FilterChip per rollFilter; EV sum with `~` prefix if any null evReduction
    94→  - EC stepper: small (42×36dp OutlinedButtons), null treated as 0.0 index, tapping value clears to null, "tap to clear" underline hint
    95→  - Aperture + Shutter: LargeStepper (52dp square OutlinedButtons + 108dp value Box); aperture list widest-first so `+`=lower index; shutter slowest-first so `+`=lower index; long-exposure shutter renders in colorScheme.error
    96→  - Notes field + OutlinedButton("Mic") → RecognizerIntent.ACTION_RECOGNIZE_SPEECH via rememberLauncherForActivityResult
    97→  - GPS: tappable "lat, lng ›" text link → geo: Intent via viewModel.onGpsCoordinatesTapped()
    98→  - BackHandler(enabled = hasUnsavedChanges) → ConfirmDiscard event → AlertDialog
    99→  - Save Changes Button → SaveSuccessful event → popBackStack()
   100→  - NavGraph.kt updated: FrameDetail route wired
   101→
   102→- `ui/quickscreen/QuickScreenScreen.kt` — complete and compiling (2026-03-20)
   103→  - Header: roll name (tappable+underlined ›  when multiple rolls loaded), active filter count, Frame X/Y, total EV (EC + filter EV sum with ~ prefix)
   104→  - Lens row: OutlinedCard, tap cycles through roll lenses (disabled if only 1 lens)
   105→  - Filter chips: FlowRow, up to 4 MRU-ordered FilterChips; "+" chip when more than 4 filters available
   106→  - Filter EV sum: right-aligned label above chip row, ~ prefix if any null evReduction
   107→  - EC SmallStepper (42×36dp buttons): null treated as 0.0 index, tapping underlined value clears to null
   108→  - Frame pointer SmallStepper: shows "X / Y"; advancing past last emits RollComplete
   109→  - Aperture LargeStepper (52dp square buttons): + = wider = lower index; − = narrower = higher index
   110→  - Shutter LargeStepper: + = slower = lower index; − = faster; long exposure renders in colorScheme.error
   111→  - Note OutlinedTextField (minLines=1, maxLines=3) + OutlinedButton("Mic") → RecognizerIntent
   112→  - Log Frame Button (64dp height, full width): GPS-aware — requests ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION if roll.gpsEnabled; uses FusedLocationProviderClient.getCurrentLocation(); falls back to null coords on denial
   113→  - FrameLogged event → HapticFeedbackType.LongPress; ConfirmOverwrite → AlertDialog; RollComplete → "Open Journal" dialog
   114→  - SwitchRollBottomSheet: list of loaded rolls, RadioButton per roll, frame progress, last-shot formatted time, LinearProgressIndicator
   115→  - FilterPickerBottomSheet: all available roll filters as toggleable FilterChips
   116→  - EmptyState: shown when no rolls loaded — "New Roll" button navigates to RollSetup
   117→  - NavGraph.kt updated: QuickScreen route wired
   118→
   119→- `ui/settings/SettingsScreen.kt` — complete and compiling (2026-03-20)
   120→  - TopAppBar: "Settings" title (no back — top-level tab)
   121→  - Sections: Shooting Defaults, Appearance, Data & Backup, Widget, Onboarding, Support, About + footer
   122→  - Extra frames per roll: stepper AlertDialog (0–10, −/+ buttons around large number)
   123→  - GPS precision, default export format, app theme: SingleChoiceDialog<T> with RadioButton list
   124→  - Accessible color mode: Switch trailing in row
   125→  - Export backup: calls ViewModel, shares via FileProvider content:// URI + Intent.ACTION_SEND
   126→  - Restore from backup: GetContent file picker → danger AlertDialog → copyUriToCache() → ViewModel
   127→  - Restart required dialog after restore: restartApp() via getLaunchIntentForPackage + finishAffinity
   128→  - Widget / Onboarding / OSS Licenses: emit events handled with snackbar TODOs (screens not yet built)
   129→  - Tip jar: OutlinedButton("tip jar ›") in SupportRow, opens TIP_JAR_URL in browser
   130→  - Privacy policy: opens PRIVACY_POLICY_URL in browser (URL placeholder — replace before release)
   131→  - Version: read from PackageManager (BuildConfig not enabled; AGP 9 disables it by default)
   132→  - NavGraph.kt updated: Settings route wired
   133→  - AndroidManifest.xml updated: ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION + ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATION permissions; FileProvider provider block
   134→  - res/xml/file_provider_paths.xml created: cache-path "backups" for backup file sharing
   135→
   136→- `ui/widget/FrameLogWidget.kt` — GlanceAppWidget + composable UI (complete, 2026-03-20)
   137→  - `WidgetState` object: all DataStore<Preferences> key definitions
   138→  - `FrameLogWidget.GlanceAppWidget` with `provideGlance` + `provideContent`
   139→  - `ActiveRollContent`: header (filmCamera, Frame X/Y, filter bullet count), divider, stepper row (aperture + shutter), divider, log frame OR roll complete bottom section
   140→  - Roll complete state: "roll complete →" in `primary` color; taps via `Intent(ACTION_VIEW, "framelog://journal/$rollId")` deep link to Roll Journal
   141→  - Log frame / roll complete branches are inline within the Column (not extracted composables) — `defaultWeight()` is a ColumnScope extension and cannot be used outside the Column lambda
   142→  - `NoRollContent`: empty state, taps to open MainActivity via explicit Intent (actionStartActivity doesn't have reified overload in glance-appwidget 1.1.0)
   143→  - `StepperButton` (28×28dp surfaceVariant background, cornerRadius 6dp), `StepperValue` (48×28dp, long-exposure in error color), `HorizontalDivider` (1dp surfaceVariant)
   144→  - Uses `GlanceTheme.colors` from `glance-appwidget` (not glance-material3) — avoids naming conflict between `androidx.glance.GlanceTheme` and `androidx.glance.material3.GlanceTheme`
   145→- `ui/widget/FrameLogWidgetReceiver.kt` — GlanceAppWidgetReceiver (complete, 2026-03-20)
   146→  - `onUpdate` and `onEnabled` call `FrameLogWidgetUpdater.update()` via MainScope coroutine
   147→- `ui/widget/FrameLogWidgetUpdater.kt` — data loader + Glance state writer (complete, 2026-03-20)
   148→  - Reads activeRollId from SharedPreferences, loads roll+filmStock+cameraBody from Room
   149→  - Writes all WidgetState keys via `updateAppWidgetState(context, glanceId) { prefs -> }` (2-arg form, provides MutablePreferences)
   150→  - Frame pointer derived entirely from Room — independent of Quick Screen's SharedPreferences pointer
   151→  - Frame pointer logic: `highestLoggedFrame` = last logged frame on the roll; `targetFrame` = first unlogged frame with frameNumber > highestLoggedFrame (or frame 1 when nothing logged); `isRollComplete` = targetFrame == null
   152→  - All exposure pre-population (aperture, shutter, LENS_ID, FILTER_IDS) sourced from `highestLoggedFrame`
   153→  - FILTER_IDS requires second DAO call (`getFrameById`) — RollWithDetails.frames contains bare Frame entities without FrameFilter join data
   154→- `ui/widget/FrameLogWidgetActions.kt` — 5 ActionCallbacks (complete, 2026-03-20)
   155→  - `ApertureUpAction` / `ApertureDownAction` — step aperture list (widest-first, + = lower index = wider)
   156→  - `ShutterUpAction` / `ShutterDownAction` — step shutter list (slowest-first, + = lower index = slower)
   157→  - `LogFrameAction` — writes frame to Room with carried-forward lens + filters (no GPS, no overwrite dialog); calls `FrameLogWidgetUpdater.update()` which re-derives frame pointer from Room; no SharedPreferences writes
   158→  - `getAppWidgetState` requires 3-arg form: `getAppWidgetState(context, PreferencesGlanceStateDefinition, glanceId): Preferences`
   159→- `AndroidManifest.xml` updated: `FrameLogWidgetReceiver` receiver; deep link intent filter (`framelog://journal`) for Roll Complete → Roll Journal navigation
   160→- `NavGraph.kt` updated: `composable<RollJournal>` has `deepLinks = listOf(navDeepLink { uriPattern = "framelog://journal/{rollId}" })`
   161→- `res/xml/frame_log_widget_info.xml` created: 4×2 target cells, 250×110dp min, updatePeriodMillis=0
   162→- `libs.versions.toml` + `app/build.gradle.kts` updated: glance 1.1.0 (glance-appwidget + glance-material3)
   163→- `QuickScreenViewModel.kt` updated: calls `FrameLogWidgetUpdater.update()` after frame log and after roll switch
   164→- `RollListViewModel.kt` updated: calls updater in `onLoadRollConfirmed()` and `onDeleteConfirmed()`
   165→- `RollJournalViewModel.kt` updated: calls updater in `onLoadRollConfirmed()`, `onFinishRollConfirmed()`, and `onDeleteConfirmed()`
   166→- `RollSetupViewModel.kt` updated: calls updater directly (suspend, not launch) in `createRoll(loadAfterCreate=true)` path
   167→
   168→**Widget state keys (WidgetState object):** HAS_ROLL, ROLL_ID, FILM_CAMERA, FRAME_NUMBER, TOTAL_FRAMES, FILTER_COUNT, APERTURE, SHUTTER, APERTURE_LIST, SHUTTER_LIST, LENS_ID (Int, -1 sentinel), FILTER_IDS (comma-separated String), IS_ROLL_COMPLETE (Boolean)
   169→
   170→**Full widget update trigger map:**
   171→| Trigger | Location |
   172→|---|---|
   173→| Widget add / launcher request | FrameLogWidgetReceiver.onUpdate/onEnabled |
   174→| Roll loaded from Roll List | RollListViewModel.onLoadRollConfirmed() |
   175→| Roll deleted from Roll List (was active) | RollListViewModel.onDeleteConfirmed() |
   176→| Roll loaded from Roll Journal | RollJournalViewModel.onLoadRollConfirmed() |
   177→| Roll finished (was active) | RollJournalViewModel.onFinishRollConfirmed() |
   178→| Roll deleted from Roll Journal (was active) | RollJournalViewModel.onDeleteConfirmed() |
   179→| New roll created and loaded | RollSetupViewModel.createRoll(loadAfterCreate=true) |
   180→| Roll switched from Quick Screen | QuickScreenViewModel.onRollSwitched() |
   181→| Frame logged from Quick Screen | QuickScreenViewModel.writeFrame() |
   182→| Frame logged from widget | LogFrameAction → FrameLogWidgetUpdater.update() |
   183→| Aperture/shutter stepped | Stepper actions — GlanceState only, no DB or updater |
   184→
   185→**Not yet started (needs user go-ahead first):**
   186→- Onboarding screens (Welcome, coach marks)
   187→
   188→---
   189→
   190→---
   191→
   192→## Bug fixes applied (2026-03-20)
   193→
   194→- **Widget frame pointer now independent of SharedPreferences** — Widget always targets first unlogged frame after the highest logged frame number (derived from Room, not SharedPrefs). No overwrite possible from widget. Roll complete state shown when no unlogged frames remain after highest logged; "roll complete →" button deep-links to Roll Journal via `framelog://journal/{rollId}`. If no frames logged yet, defaults to frame 1. Skipped frames (e.g. frame 6 skipped via Quick Screen, frames 7+ logged) are correctly bypassed — widget advances past them.
   195→
   196→- **Widget logged frames with null lens and empty filters** — `LogFrameAction` used `frame.lensId` (always null on fresh/unlogged frame slots) and `emptyList()` for filters. Fixed: `FrameLogWidgetUpdater` now stores `LENS_ID` and `FILTER_IDS` in GlanceState from the last logged frame. `LogFrameAction` reads these, uses `lensId` from state, and computes the filter delta (reads existing FrameFilter rows defensively to handle the silent-overwrite case, then diffs against `targetFilterIds`).
   197→
   198→- **Widget didn't update when rolls were loaded/finished/deleted outside Quick Screen** — `RollListViewModel`, `RollJournalViewModel`, and `RollSetupViewModel` had no widget update calls. Fixed: all three now call `FrameLogWidgetUpdater.update(getApplication())` at every roll state transition that could change what the widget displays.
   199→
   200→- **NavGraph.kt window insets regression** — `consumeWindowInsets(innerPadding)` was consuming both top and bottom, hiding FABs behind the nav bar and causing status bar overlap. Fixed: apply only `padding(bottom = innerPadding.calculateBottomPadding())` + `consumeWindowInsets(WindowInsets(bottom = ...))`, leaving top insets for inner Scaffolds' TopAppBars.
   201→
   202→- **Crash: deleting an active/loaded roll** — `RollDao.getRollById` returned `Flow<RollWithDetails>` (non-nullable); when the roll was deleted, Room emitted `null`, causing NPE at `collect`. Fixed: return type changed to `Flow<RollWithDetails?>` in both DAO and RollRepository. Added null guard + `NavigateBack` event in `RollJournalViewModel`. Added `?: return` guard in `FrameDetailViewModel.populateFromFrame`.
   203→
   204→- **Crash: "Load from kit" in RollSetupScreen** — shared ViewModel via `getBackStackEntry(RollSetup::class)` is unreliable in Navigation Compose. Replaced entirely: `KitSelectorScreen` now writes kitId to `previousBackStackEntry?.savedStateHandle`; `RollSetupViewModel` observes that key via `getStateFlow` (see KitSelectorScreen entry above).
   205→
   206→## Bottom Navigation — wired (2026-03-20)
   207→
   208→- Root `Scaffold` in `NavGraph.kt` hosts `FrameLogNavigationBar` (private composable)
   209→- `Modifier.consumeWindowInsets(innerPadding)` on the `NavHost` Box — prevents double-padding with per-screen Scaffolds
   210→- Start destination changed from `GearLibrary` → `QuickScreen`
   211→- Bottom bar shown only on the 5 top-level routes (GearLibrary, RollList, QuickScreen, RollJournal, Settings); hidden on all detail screens
   212→- Tab 4 (Journal): reads `AppPreferences.activeRollId` at tap time; falls back to RollList if no roll loaded
   213→- All tab navigations use `popUpTo<QuickScreen> + launchSingleTop + restoreState`; Quick tab itself pops inclusive
   214→- Icons: Build (Gear), AutoMirrored.Filled.List (Rolls), Home (Quick), Create (Journal), Settings (Settings)
   215→
   216→## Architecture notes
   217→
   218→- No DI framework — repositories get AppDatabase passed directly
   219→- Navigation: type-safe routes via @Serializable data classes; SavedStateHandle auto-populated with route params by Navigation 2.8.0
   220→- ViewModel creation: `viewModel()` inside `composable<T>` — Navigation Compose handles Application + SavedStateHandle injection automatically
   221→- Start destination is GearLibrary temporarily; will move to QuickScreen with onboarding/launch logic later
   222→- MediumFormat excluded from all v1.0 UI (CameraBodyFormat, FilmFormat)
   223→- `collectAsStateWithLifecycle()` used in screen composables (requires lifecycle-runtime-compose; available via lifecycle 2.8.0 BOM)
   224→
   225→**Why:** App is offline-first, no server. data/model/ dir not created — entities serve as models directly.
   226→**How to apply:** Wait for explicit go-ahead from user before starting large implementation blocks. Repository layer is the correct access point for ViewModels.
   227→

<system-reminder>

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

file.content:
---
name: FrameLog implementation status
description: Full implementation status of the FRAME//LOG Android app — data, VM, and UI layers
type: project
---

## Fully complete layers (compiling cleanly as of 2026-03-19)

**Database / data layer:**
- All 12 Room entities in `data/db/entity/`
- All enums in `Enums.kt`: CameraBodyFormat, FilmFormat, ShutterIncrements, ApertureIncrements, ColorType, RollStatus
- TypeConverters for all enums (string values, not ordinals)
- All 11 DAOs in `data/db/dao/`
- Relation types in `data/db/relation/`: RollWithDetails, FrameWithDetails, KitWithDetails, + join relations
- Export types in `RollExport.kt`
- AppDatabase: singleton, all DAO accessors, 3 multi-DAO @Transaction methods
- All 4 repositories in `data/repository/`

**Infrastructure:**
- `data/AppPreferences.kt` — SharedPreferences wrapper (activeRollId, onboarding, settings, per-roll currentFrame + MRU filters)
- `ui/util/ExposureValues.kt` — shutter speed + aperture value lists, display formatters, EC values
- `ui/util/ExportFormatter.kt` — CSV, JSON, plain text formatters for RollExport

**ViewModel layer (13 VMs):**
GearLibraryViewModel, LensDetailViewModel, CameraBodyDetailViewModel, FilterDetailViewModel,
FilmStockDetailViewModel, KitDetailViewModel, KitSelectorViewModel, RollListViewModel,
RollSetupViewModel, RollJournalViewModel, FrameDetailViewModel, QuickScreenViewModel, SettingsViewModel

---

## UI layer — Gear Library screens complete (verified compiling as of 2026-03-20)

**Done and compiling clean:**
- `ui/navigation/NavRoutes.kt` — all type-safe @Serializable route definitions (Navigation Compose 2.8.0)
- `ui/navigation/NavGraph.kt` — FrameLogNavGraph NavHost; all Gear Library routes wired, Roll/Quick/Settings routes commented as TODO
- `MainActivity.kt` — updated to use FrameLogNavGraph + rememberNavController
- `ui/gear/GearLibraryScreen.kt` — full 5-tab implementation (Lenses, Bodies, Filters, Film, Kits); ScrollableTabRow, search + sort dropdown, LazyColumn, gear cards for all types, EmptyState; defines ColorType.label (internal)
- `ui/gear/LensDetailScreen.kt` — full form; defines shared internal composables **MountTypeField** and **EnumDropdown**, plus ApertureIncrements.label
- `ui/gear/CameraBodyDetailScreen.kt` — full form; defines CameraBodyFormat.label and ShutterIncrements.label
- `ui/gear/FilterDetailScreen.kt` — full form; private FilterTypeField autocomplete composable
- `ui/gear/FilmStockDetailScreen.kt` — full form with discontinued Switch; defines FilmFormat.label; reuses ColorType.label from GearLibraryScreen.kt
- `ui/gear/KitDetailScreen.kt` — full form: body picker, lens add/remove/primary-select, filter toggles, IncompatibleLensesRemoved snackbar, Duplicate text button

**Roll screens — in progress:**
- `ui/rolls/RollListScreen.kt` — complete and compiling (2026-03-20)
  - 3-tab layout (Active/Finished/Archived) with counts
  - Active tab splits into "In camera" (loaded) and "Inventory" (unloaded) sections
  - SwipeToDismissBox on unloaded cards → load confirmation dialog
  - Long press context menu (state-aware options: Load Roll, Open Journal, Archive, Unarchive, Delete)
  - 4 AlertDialog confirmations: Load, Delete, Archive, Unarchive
  - `RollListRow` data class (JOIN: Roll + filmStock name + cameraBody name + loggedFrameCount)
  - `dashedBorder` modifier extension for unloaded card treatment
  - `NavGraph.kt` updated: RollList route wired
- `ui/rolls/RollJournalScreen.kt` — complete and compiling (2026-03-20)
  - TopAppBar (back, roll name, overflow ⋯ with Archive/Unarchive/Delete)
  - Header: ISO · push/pull · loaded status subtitle + LinearProgressIndicator (logged/total)
  - Search OutlinedTextField + sort dropdown (frame 1→N / N→1) via KeyboardArrowDown/Up
  - LazyColumn of FrameCards: logged (solid border, filled circle, aperture/shutter/lens/EC/GPS/notes) and unlogged (dashed border, empty circle, "— unlogged —"); current frame gets tertiary accent border + "CURRENT" label
  - Long-exposure shutter speeds render in `colorScheme.error` (red convention)
  - Bottom bar: unloaded active → Load Roll + Export; loaded active → Finish Roll + Export; finished/archived → Export only
  - ExportFormatBottomSheet (ModalBottomSheet): CSV/JSON/Plain Text RadioButton list + Export button
  - 5 AlertDialog confirmations: Load, Finish Roll (error-colored, irreversible warning), Archive, Unarchive, Delete (error-colored)
  - Share export via `Intent.ACTION_SEND` (content as EXTRA_TEXT)
  - `NavGraph.kt` updated: RollJournal route wired

- `ui/rolls/RollSetupScreen.kt` — complete and compiling (2026-03-20)
  - Scrollable form: FilmStock, Kit (optional), CameraBody, Lenses, Filters, Details, CustomISO, Actions
  - ModalBottomSheet pickers for FilmStock, CameraBody, AddLens (filtered by mount type)

**Full widget update trigger map:**
| Trigger | Location |
|---|---|
| Widget add / launcher request | FrameLogWidgetReceiver.onUpdate/onEnabled |
| Roll loaded from Roll List | RollListViewModel.onLoadRollConfirmed() |
| Roll deleted from Roll List (was active) | RollListViewModel.onDeleteConfirmed() |
| Roll loaded from Roll Journal | RollJournalViewModel.onLoadRollConfirmed() |
| Roll finished (was active) | RollJournalViewModel.onFinishRollConfirmed() |
| Roll deleted from Roll Journal (was active) | RollJournalViewModel.onDeleteConfirmed() |
| New roll created and loaded | RollSetupViewModel.createRoll(loadAfterCreate=true) |
| Roll switched from Quick Screen | QuickScreenViewModel.onRollSwitched() |
| Frame logged from Quick Screen | QuickScreenViewModel.writeFrame() |
| Frame logged from widget | LogFrameAction → FrameLogWidgetUpdater.update() |
| Aperture/shutter stepped | Stepper actions — GlanceState only, no DB or updater |

**Not yet started (needs user go-ahead first):**
- Onboarding screens (Welcome, coach marks)

---

---

## Bug fixes applied (2026-03-20)

- **Widget frame pointer now independent of SharedPreferences** — Widget always targets first unlogged frame after the highest logged frame number (derived from Room, not SharedPrefs). No overwrite possible from widget. Roll complete state shown when no unlogged frames remain after highest logged; "roll complete →" button deep-links to Roll Journal via `framelog://journal/{rollId}`. If no frames logged yet, defaults to frame 1. Skipped frames (e.g. frame 6 skipped via Quick Screen, frames 7+ logged) are correctly bypassed — widget advances past them.

- **Widget logged frames with null lens and empty filters** — `LogFrameAction` used `frame.lensId` (always null on fresh/unlogged frame slots) and `emptyList()` for filters. Fixed: `FrameLogWidgetUpdater` now stores `LENS_ID` and `FILTER_IDS` in GlanceState from the last logged frame. `LogFrameAction` reads these, uses `lensId` from state, and computes the filter delta (reads existing FrameFilter rows defensively to handle the silent-overwrite case, then diffs against `targetFilterIds`).

- **Widget didn't update when rolls were loaded/finished/deleted outside Quick Screen** — `RollListViewModel`, `RollJournalViewModel`, and `RollSetupViewModel` had no widget update calls. Fixed: all three now call `FrameLogWidgetUpdater.update(getApplication())` at every roll state transition that could change what the widget displays.

- **NavGraph.kt window insets regression** — `consumeWindowInsets(innerPadding)` was consuming both top and bottom, hiding FABs behind the nav bar and causing status bar overlap. Fixed: apply only `padding(bottom = innerPadding.calculateBottomPadding())` + `consumeWindowInsets(WindowInsets(bottom = ...))`, leaving top insets for inner Scaffolds' TopAppBars.

- **Crash: deleting an active/loaded roll** — `RollDao.getRollById` returned `Flow<RollWithDetails>` (non-nullable); when the roll was deleted, Room emitted `null`, causing NPE at `collect`. Fixed: return type changed to `Flow<RollWithDetails?>` in both DAO and RollRepository. Added null guard + `NavigateBack` event in `RollJournalViewModel`. Added `?: return` guard in `FrameDetailViewModel.populateFromFrame`.

- **Crash: "Load from kit" in RollSetupScreen** — shared ViewModel via `getBackStackEntry(RollSetup::class)` is unreliable in Navigation Compose. Replaced entirely: `KitSelectorScreen` now writes kitId to `previousBackStackEntry?.savedStateHandle`; `RollSetupViewModel` observes that key via `getStateFlow` (see KitSelectorScreen entry above).

## Bottom Navigation — wired (2026-03-20)

- Root `Scaffold` in `NavGraph.kt` hosts `FrameLogNavigationBar` (private composable)
- `Modifier.consumeWindowInsets(innerPadding)` on the `NavHost` Box — prevents double-padding with per-screen Scaffolds
- Start destination changed from `GearLibrary` → `QuickScreen`
- Bottom bar shown only on the 5 top-level routes (GearLibrary, RollList, QuickScreen, RollJournal, Settings); hidden on all detail screens
- Tab 4 (Journal): reads `AppPreferences.activeRollId` at tap time; falls back to RollList if no roll loaded
- All tab navigations use `popUpTo<QuickScreen> + launchSingleTop + restoreState`; Quick tab itself pops inclusive
- Icons: Build (Gear), AutoMirrored.Filled.List (Rolls), Home (Quick), Create (Journal), Settings (Settings)

## Architecture notes

- No DI framework — repositories get AppDatabase passed directly
- Navigation: type-safe routes via @Serializable data classes; SavedStateHandle auto-populated with route params by Navigation 2.8.0
- ViewModel creation: `viewModel()` inside `composable<T>` — Navigation Compose handles Application + SavedStateHandle injection automatically
- Start destination is GearLibrary temporarily; will move to QuickScreen with onboarding/launch logic later
- MediumFormat excluded from all v1.0 UI (CameraBodyFormat, FilmFormat)
- `collectAsStateWithLifecycle()` used in screen composables (requires lifecycle-runtime-compose; available via lifecycle 2.8.0 BOM)

**Why:** App is offline-first, no server. data/model/ dir not created — entities serve as models directly.
**How to apply:** Wait for explicit go-ahead from user before starting large implementation blocks. Repository layer is the correct access point for ViewModels.

     6→

     8→

    10→- ViewModel's `savedState: SavedStateHandle` — created by `AbstractSavedStateViewModelFactory` for the ViewModel's own state. Receives navigation route arguments at creation time.

file.content:
---
name: NavBackStackEntry.savedStateHandle vs ViewModel savedState are different objects
description: Critical Navigation Compose architectural fact — the navigation-results handle and the ViewModel's SavedStateHandle are not the same object
type: feedback
---

`NavBackStackEntry.savedStateHandle` (used for passing results between screens) is a **different object** from the `SavedStateHandle` injected into a ViewModel via its constructor.

- `NavBackStackEntry.savedStateHandle` — backed by `NavResultSavedStateViewModel`; this is the navigation-results handle. `navController.previousBackStackEntry?.savedStateHandle` writes to this.
- ViewModel's `savedState: SavedStateHandle` — created by `AbstractSavedStateViewModelFactory` for the ViewModel's own state. Receives navigation route arguments at creation time.

Writing to one has NO effect on the other.

**Why:** Discovered when `RollSetupViewModel.observeKitSelection()` used `savedState.getStateFlow("selected_kit_id", -1)` to receive a kit ID written by `KitSelectorScreen` via `navController.previousBackStackEntry?.savedStateHandle`. The ViewModel's collect block never fired because it was watching the wrong object.

**How to apply:** For the "return a result to previous screen" pattern in Navigation Compose:
- The **sending** screen writes to `navController.previousBackStackEntry?.savedStateHandle?.set(key, value)`
- The **receiving** screen observes via `navController.currentBackStackEntry?.savedStateHandle?.getStateFlow(key, default)` **in the composable** (e.g., a `LaunchedEffect`), NOT in the ViewModel
- The ViewModel exposes a regular function (e.g., `loadAndApplyKit(kitId)`) that the composable calls when the value arrives

    31→import androidx.compose.material3.AlertDialog
    32→import androidx.compose.material3.Button
    33→import androidx.compose.material3.CircularProgressIndicator
    34→import androidx.compose.material3.ExperimentalMaterial3Api
    35→import androidx.compose.material3.FilterChip
    36→import androidx.compose.material3.HorizontalDivider
    37→import androidx.compose.material3.Icon
    38→import androidx.compose.material3.IconButton
    39→import androidx.compose.material3.ListItem
    40→import androidx.compose.material3.MaterialTheme
    41→import androidx.compose.material3.ModalBottomSheet
    42→import androidx.compose.material3.OutlinedButton
    43→import androidx.compose.material3.OutlinedTextField
    44→import androidx.compose.material3.Scaffold
    45→import androidx.compose.material3.SnackbarHost
    46→import androidx.compose.material3.SnackbarHostState
    47→import androidx.compose.material3.Text
    48→import androidx.compose.material3.TextButton
    49→import androidx.compose.material3.TopAppBar
    50→import androidx.compose.material3.rememberModalBottomSheetState
    51→import androidx.compose.runtime.Composable
    52→import androidx.compose.runtime.LaunchedEffect
    53→import androidx.compose.runtime.getValue
    54→import androidx.compose.runtime.mutableStateOf
    55→import androidx.compose.runtime.remember
    56→import androidx.compose.runtime.setValue
    57→import androidx.compose.ui.Alignment
    58→import androidx.compose.ui.Modifier
    59→import androidx.compose.ui.graphics.Color
    60→import androidx.compose.ui.platform.LocalContext
    61→import androidx.compose.ui.text.style.TextAlign
    62→import androidx.compose.ui.text.style.TextDecoration
    63→import androidx.compose.ui.unit.dp
    64→import androidx.lifecycle.compose.collectAsStateWithLifecycle
    65→import androidx.lifecycle.viewmodel.compose.viewModel
    66→import androidx.navigation.NavHostController
    67→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.db.entity.Filter
    68→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.db.entity.Lens
    69→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.ui.util.ExposureValues
    70→import java.text.SimpleDateFormat
    71→import java.util.Date
    72→import java.util.Locale
    73→import kotlin.math.abs
    74→
    75→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    76→// Screen
    77→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    78→
    79→@OptIn(ExperimentalMaterial3Api::class, ExperimentalLayoutApi::class)
    80→@Composable
    81→fun FrameDetailScreen(navController: NavHostController) {
    82→    val viewModel: FrameDetailViewModel = viewModel()
    83→    val state by viewModel.state.collectAsStateWithLifecycle()
    84→    val context = LocalContext.current
    85→
    86→    var showDiscardDialog by remember { mutableStateOf(false) }
    87→    var showLensPicker by remember { mutableStateOf(false) }
    88→    val snackbarHostState = remember { SnackbarHostState() }
    89→
    90→    // Speech recognition: append recognized text into the note field
    91→    val speechLauncher = rememberLauncherForActivityResult(
    92→        contract = ActivityResultContracts.StartActivityForResult(),
    93→    ) { result ->
    94→        val matches = result.data?.getStringArrayListExtra(RecognizerIntent.EXTRA_RESULTS)
    95→        matches?.firstOrNull()?.let { viewModel.onNoteChanged(it) }
    96→    }
    97→
    98→    // Intercept system back when there are unsaved changes
    99→    BackHandler(enabled = state.hasUnsavedChanges) {
   100→        viewModel.onBackPressed()
   101→    }
   102→
   103→    // One-shot event handler
   104→    LaunchedEffect(Unit) {
   105→        viewModel.events.collect { event ->
   106→            when (event) {
   107→                FrameDetailEvent.SaveSuccessful ->
   108→                    navController.popBackStack()
   109→                FrameDetailEvent.ConfirmDiscard ->
   110→                    showDiscardDialog = true
   111→                is FrameDetailEvent.OpenMapIntent -> {
   112→                    // Open the system maps app at the recorded GPS coordinates
   113→                    val uri = Uri.parse(
   114→                        "geo:${event.lat},${event.lng}?q=${event.lat},${event.lng}"
   115→                    )
   116→                    context.startActivity(Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW, uri))
   117→                }
   118→            }
   119→        }
   120→    }
   121→
   122→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   123→    // Derived display values
   124→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   125→
   126→    // Order filters: MRU chips first, then remaining roll filters
   127→    val orderedFilters: List<Filter> = remember(state.mruFilterIds, state.rollFilters) {
   128→        val mruFilters = state.mruFilterIds
   129→            .mapNotNull { id -> state.rollFilters.firstOrNull { it.id == id } }
   130→        val restFilters = state.rollFilters.filterNot { it.id in state.mruFilterIds }
   131→        mruFilters + restFilters
   132→    }
   133→
   134→    // EV sum for active filters, with "~" prefix when any active filter has null evReduction
   135→    val evSumDisplay: String? = remember(state.activeFilterIds, state.rollFilters) {
   136→        val active = state.rollFilters.filter { it.id in state.activeFilterIds }
   137→        if (active.isEmpty()) return@remember null
   138→        val hasUnknownEv = active.any { it.evReduction == null }
   139→        val sum = active.fold(0f) { acc, f -> acc + (f.evReduction ?: 0f) }
   140→        val prefix = if (hasUnknownEv) "~" else ""
   141→        "${prefix}${ExposureValues.formatExposureCompensation(sum)} EV"
   142→    }
   143→
   144→    // Shutter speed and aperture stepper indices
   145→    val apertureIndex = state.availableApertures.indexOf(state.aperture)
   146→    val shutterIndex = state.availableShutterSpeeds.indexOf(state.shutterSpeed)
   147→
   148→    // EC stepper: treat null as pointing at the 0 EV position
   149→    val ecValues = ExposureValues.exposureCompensationValues
   150→    val ecZeroIndex = remember(ecValues) {
   151→        ecValues.indexOfFirst { abs(it) < 0.001f }.takeIf { it >= 0 } ?: (ecValues.size / 2)
   152→    }
   153→    val ecIndex = state.exposureCompensation
   154→        ?.let { v -> ecValues.indexOfFirst { abs(it - v) < 0.01f }.takeIf { it >= 0 } }
   155→        ?: ecZeroIndex
   156→
   157→    // Subtitle: timestamp + GPS indicator (film stock / body name not available in this VM)
   158→    val dateTimeFormatter = remember { SimpleDateFormat("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm", Locale.getDefault()) }
   159→    val subtitle: String? = remember(state.frame) {
   160→        val frame = state.frame?.frame ?: return@remember null
   161→        buildString {
   162→            frame.loggedAt?.let { append(dateTimeFormatter.format(Date(it))) }
   163→            if (frame.lat != null && frame.lng != null) {
   164→                if (isNotEmpty()) append(" \u00b7 ")
   165→                append("GPS logged")
   166→            }
   167→        }.ifBlank { null }
   168→    }
   169→
   170→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   171→    // Scaffold
   172→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   173→
   174→    Scaffold(
   175→        topBar = {
   176→            TopAppBar(
   177→                title = {
   178→                    Column {
   179→                        Text(
   180→                            text = "Frame ${state.frame?.frame?.frameNumber ?: ""}",
   181→                            style = MaterialTheme.typography.titleMedium,
   182→                        )
   183→                        if (subtitle != null) {
   184→                            Text(
   185→                                text = subtitle,
   186→                                style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodySmall,
   187→                                color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant,
   188→                            )
   189→                        }
   190→                    }
   191→                },
   192→                navigationIcon = {
   193→                    IconButton(
   194→                        onClick = {
   195→                            if (state.hasUnsavedChanges) viewModel.onBackPressed()
   196→                            else navController.popBackStack()
   197→                        },
   198→                    ) {
   199→                        Icon(Icons.AutoMirrored.Filled.ArrowBack, contentDescription = "Back")
   200→                    }
   201→                },
   202→            )
   203→        },
   204→        snackbarHost = { SnackbarHost(snackbarHostState) },
   205→    ) { innerPadding ->
   206→
   207→        if (state.isLoading) {
   208→            Box(
   209→                modifier = Modifier.fillMaxSize().padding(innerPadding),
   210→                contentAlignment = Alignment.Center,
   211→            ) {
   212→                CircularProgressIndicator()
   213→            }
   214→            return@Scaffold
   215→        }
   216→
   217→        Column(
   218→            modifier = Modifier
   219→                .fillMaxSize()
   220→                .padding(innerPadding)
   221→                .verticalScroll(rememberScrollState()),
   222→        ) {
   223→
   224→            // ==================================================================
   225→            // Logged toggle
   226→            // ==================================================================
   227→
   228→            HorizontalDivider()
   229→            Row(
   230→                modifier = Modifier
   231→                    .fillMaxWidth()
   232→                    .padding(horizontal = 16.dp, vertical = 10.dp),
   233→                verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically,
   234→                horizontalArrangement = Arrangement.spacedBy(12.dp),
   235→            ) {
   236→                Text(
   237→                    text = "logged",
   238→                    style = MaterialTheme.typography.labelSmall,
   239→                    color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant,
   240→                    modifier = Modifier.width(48.dp),
   241→                )
   242→                FilterChip(
   243→                    selected = state.isLogged,
   244→                    onClick = { viewModel.onIsLoggedChanged(!state.isLogged) },
   245→                    label = { Text(if (state.isLogged) "yes" else "no") },
   246→                )
   247→                if (state.isLogged) {
   248→                    Text(
   249→                        text = "tap to mark unlogged",
   250→                        style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodySmall,
   251→                        color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant,
   252→                    )
   253→                }
   254→            }
   255→            HorizontalDivider()
   256→
   257→            // ==================================================================
   258→            // Lens
   259→            // ==================================================================
   260→
   261→            Spacer(Modifier.height(4.dp))
   262→
   263→            // Lens picker row
   264→            val selectedLensName = state.rollLenses
   265→                .firstOrNull { it.id == state.selectedLensId }
   266→                ?.name
   267→                ?: if (state.rollLenses.isEmpty()) "No lenses on this roll" else "Select lens"
   268→
   269→            Row(
   270→                modifier = Modifier
   271→                    .fillMaxWidth()
   272→                    .then(
   273→                        if (state.rollLenses.isNotEmpty())
   274→                            Modifier.clickable { showLensPicker = true }
   275→                        else Modifier
   276→                    )
   277→                    .padding(horizontal = 16.dp, vertical = 10.dp),
   278→                verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically,
   279→            ) {
   280→                Text(
   281→                    text = "lens",
   282→                    style = MaterialTheme.typography.labelSmall,
   283→                    color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant,
   284→                    modifier = Modifier.width(48.dp),
   285→                )
   286→                Text(
   287→                    text = selectedLensName,
   288→                    style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyMedium,
   289→                    modifier = Modifier.weight(1f),
   290→                )
   291→                if (state.rollLenses.isNotEmpty()) {
   292→                    Text(
   293→                        text = "\u203a", // ›
   294→                        style = MaterialTheme.typography.titleMedium,
   295→                        color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant,
   296→                    )
   297→                }
   298→            }
   299→
   300→            // ==================================================================
   301→            // Filters
   302→            // ==================================================================
   303→
   304→            if (state.rollFilters.isNotEmpty()) {
   305→                HorizontalDivider(modifier = Modifier.padding(horizontal = 0.dp))
   306→                Row(
   307→                    modifier = Modifier
   308→                        .fillMaxWidth()
   309→                        .padding(horizontal = 16.dp, vertical = 8.dp),
   310→                    verticalAlignment = Alignment.Top,
   311→                ) {
   312→                    Text(
   313→                        text = "filters",
   314→                        style = MaterialTheme.typography.labelSmall,
   315→                        color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant,
   316→                        modifier = Modifier.width(48.dp).padding(top = 10.dp),
   317→                    )
   318→                    FlowRow(
   319→                        modifier = Modifier.weight(1f),
   320→                        horizontalArrangement = Arrangement.spacedBy(8.dp),
   321→                    ) {
   322→                        orderedFilters.forEach { filter ->
   323→                            FilterChip(
   324→                                selected = filter.id in state.activeFilterIds,
   325→                                onClick = { viewModel.onFilterToggled(filter.id) },
   326→                                label = { Text(filter.name) },
   327→                            )
   328→                        }
   329→                    }
   330→                    if (evSumDisplay != null) {
   331→                        Text(
   332→                            text = evSumDisplay,
   333→                            style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodySmall,
   334→                            color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant,
   335→                            modifier = Modifier.padding(top = 10.dp, start = 8.dp),
   336→                        )
   337→                    }
   338→                }
   339→            }
   340→
   341→            HorizontalDivider()
   342→
   343→            // ==================================================================
   344→            // Exposure compensation (centered, small stepper)
   345→            // ==================================================================
   346→
   347→            Spacer(Modifier.height(8.dp))
   348→            Text(
   349→                text = "exposure compensation",
   350→                style = MaterialTheme.typography.labelSmall,
   351→                color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant,
   352→                modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth(),
   353→                textAlign = TextAlign.Center,
   354→            )
   355→            Spacer(Modifier.height(4.dp))
   356→
   357→            Row(
   358→                modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth(),
   359→                horizontalArrangement = Arrangement.Center,
   360→                verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically,
   361→            ) {
   362→                // − button
   363→                OutlinedButton(
   364→                    onClick = {
   365→                        val newIdx = (ecIndex - 1).coerceAtLeast(0)
   366→                        viewModel.onExposureCompensationChanged(ecValues[newIdx])
   367→                    },
   368→                    enabled = ecIndex > 0,
   369→                    modifier = Modifier.height(36.dp).width(42.dp),
   370→                    contentPadding = PaddingValues(0.dp),
   371→                ) {
   372→                    Text("\u2212")
   373→                }
   374→
   375→                // EC value display — tap to clear
   376→                Box(
   377→                    modifier = Modifier
   378→                        .padding(horizontal = 8.dp)
   379→                        .width(80.dp)
   380→                        .clickable(enabled = state.exposureCompensation != null) {
   381→                            viewModel.onExposureCompensationChanged(null)
   382→                        },
   383→                    contentAlignment = Alignment.Center,
   384→                ) {
   385→                    val ecDisplay = state.exposureCompensation
   386→                        ?.let { "${ExposureValues.formatExposureCompensation(it)} EV" }
   387→                        ?: "0 EV"
   388→                    val ecColor = if (state.exposureCompensation == null)
   389→                        MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant
   390→                    else
   391→                        MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurface
   392→                    Column(horizontalAlignment = Alignment.CenterHorizontally) {
   393→                        Text(
   394→                            text = ecDisplay,
   395→                            style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyLarge,
   396→                            color = ecColor,
   397→                            textAlign = TextAlign.Center,
   398→                        )
   399→                        if (state.exposureCompensation != null) {
   400→                            Text(
   401→                                text = "tap to clear",
   402→                                style = MaterialTheme.typography.labelSmall,
   403→                                color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant,
   404→                                textDecoration = TextDecoration.Underline,
   405→                            )
   406→                        }
   407→                    }
   408→                }
   409→
   410→                // + button
   411→                OutlinedButton(
   412→                    onClick = {
   413→                        val newIdx = (ecIndex + 1).coerceAtMost(ecValues.size - 1)
   414→                        viewModel.onExposureCompensationChanged(ecValues[newIdx])
   415→                    },
   416→                    enabled = ecIndex < ecValues.size - 1,
   417→                    modifier = Modifier.height(36.dp).width(42.dp),
   418→                    contentPadding = PaddingValues(0.dp),
   419→                ) {
   420→                    Text("+")
   421→                }
   422→            }
   423→
   424→            Spacer(Modifier.height(8.dp))
   425→            HorizontalDivider()
   426→
   427→            // ==================================================================
   428→            // Aperture stepper (large, centered)
   429→            // ==================================================================
   430→
   431→            Spacer(Modifier.height(12.dp))
   432→            Text(
   433→                text = "aperture",
   434→                style = MaterialTheme.typography.labelSmall,
   435→                color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant,
   436→                modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth(),
   437→                textAlign = TextAlign.Center,
   438→            )
   439→            Spacer(Modifier.height(6.dp))
   440→
   441→            // Aperture list is widest→narrowest (index 0 = widest = f/1.4).
   442→            // + = wider aperture (lower index), − = narrower aperture (higher index).
   443→            LargeStepper(
   444→                value = state.aperture ?: "—",
   445→                onDecrement = {
   446→                    // Narrower: step to higher index
   447→                    val next = state.availableApertures.getOrNull(apertureIndex + 1)
   448→                    if (next != null) viewModel.onApertureChanged(next)
   449→                },
   450→                onIncrement = {
   451→                    // Wider: step to lower index
   452→                    val prev = state.availableApertures.getOrNull(apertureIndex - 1)
   453→                    if (prev != null) viewModel.onApertureChanged(prev)
   454→                },
   455→                canDecrement = apertureIndex < state.availableApertures.size - 1,
   456→                canIncrement = apertureIndex > 0,
   457→            )
   458→
   459→            Spacer(Modifier.height(12.dp))
   460→
   461→            // ==================================================================
   462→            // Shutter speed stepper (large, centered)
   463→            // ==================================================================
   464→
   465→            Text(
   466→                text = "shutter speed",
   467→                style = MaterialTheme.typography.labelSmall,
   468→                color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant,
   469→                modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth(),
   470→                textAlign = TextAlign.Center,
   471→            )
   472→            Spacer(Modifier.height(6.dp))
   473→
   474→            // Shutter list is slowest→fastest (index 0 = B = slowest).
   475→            // + = slower shutter (lower index = more light), − = faster shutter (higher index).
   476→            val shutterDisplay = state.shutterSpeed
   477→                ?.let { ExposureValues.shutterDisplayValue(it) }
   478→                ?: "—"
   479→            val isLongExposure = state.shutterSpeed
   480→                ?.let { ExposureValues.isLongExposure(it) }
   481→                ?: false
   482→
   483→            LargeStepper(
   484→                value = shutterDisplay,
   485→                onDecrement = {
   486→                    // Faster shutter: step to higher index
   487→                    val next = state.availableShutterSpeeds.getOrNull(shutterIndex + 1)
   488→                    if (next != null) viewModel.onShutterSpeedChanged(next)
   489→                },
   490→                onIncrement = {
   491→                    // Slower shutter: step to lower index
   492→                    val prev = state.availableShutterSpeeds.getOrNull(shutterIndex - 1)
   493→                    if (prev != null) viewModel.onShutterSpeedChanged(prev)
   494→                },
   495→                canDecrement = shutterIndex < state.availableShutterSpeeds.size - 1,
   496→                canIncrement = shutterIndex > 0,
   497→                // Whole-second values and bulb render in error/accent color per film camera convention
   498→                valueColor = if (isLongExposure) MaterialTheme.colorScheme.error
   499→                             else MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurface,
   500→            )
   501→
   502→            Spacer(Modifier.height(12.dp))
   503→            HorizontalDivider()
   504→
   505→            // ==================================================================
   506→            // Note + mic
   507→            // ==================================================================
   508→
   509→            Row(
   510→                modifier = Modifier
   511→                    .fillMaxWidth()
   512→                    .padding(horizontal = 16.dp, vertical = 8.dp),
   513→                horizontalArrangement = Arrangement.spacedBy(8.dp),
   514→                verticalAlignment = Alignment.Top,
   515→            ) {
   516→                OutlinedTextField(
   517→                    value = state.note,
   518→                    onValueChange = viewModel::onNoteChanged,
   519→                    modifier = Modifier.weight(1f),
   520→                    label = { Text("note") },
   521→                    minLines = 3,
   522→                    maxLines = 5,
   523→                )
   524→                OutlinedButton(
   525→                    onClick = {
   526→                        val intent = Intent(RecognizerIntent.ACTION_RECOGNIZE_SPEECH).apply {
   527→                            putExtra(
   528→                                RecognizerIntent.EXTRA_LANGUAGE_MODEL,
   529→                                RecognizerIntent.LANGUAGE_MODEL_FREE_FORM,
   530→                            )
   531→                            putExtra(RecognizerIntent.EXTRA_PROMPT, "Add a note for this frame")
   532→                        }
   533→                        try {
   534→                            speechLauncher.launch(intent)
   535→                        } catch (_: Exception) {
   536→                            // Speech recognition not available on this device
   537→                        }
   538→                    },
   539→                    modifier = Modifier
   540→                        .height(88.dp)
   541→                        .width(52.dp),
   542→                    contentPadding = PaddingValues(horizontal = 4.dp, vertical = 0.dp),
   543→                ) {
   544→                    Text(
   545→                        text = "Mic",
   546→                        style = MaterialTheme.typography.labelSmall,
   547→                        textAlign = TextAlign.Center,
   548→                    )
   549→                }
   550→            }
   551→
   552→            HorizontalDivider()
   553→
   554→            // ==================================================================
   555→            // Meta: logged timestamp + GPS coordinates
   556→            // ==================================================================
   557→
   558→            Column(modifier = Modifier.padding(horizontal = 16.dp, vertical = 10.dp)) {
   559→                val loggedAtDisplay = state.frame?.frame?.loggedAt
   560→                    ?.let { dateTimeFormatter.format(Date(it)) }
   561→                if (loggedAtDisplay != null) {
   562→                    Text(
   563→                        text = "logged $loggedAtDisplay",
   564→                        style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodySmall,
   565→                        color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant,
   566→                    )
   567→                }
   568→
   569→                val lat = state.frame?.frame?.lat
   570→                val lng = state.frame?.frame?.lng
   571→                if (lat != null && lng != null) {
   572→                    val coordText = "%.4f, %.4f".format(lat, lng)
   573→                    Text(
   574→                        text = "$coordText  \u203a",
   575→                        style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodySmall.copy(
   576→                            textDecoration = TextDecoration.Underline,
   577→                        ),
   578→                        color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary,
   579→                        modifier = Modifier
   580→                            .padding(top = 4.dp)
   581→                            .clickable { viewModel.onGpsCoordinatesTapped() },
   582→                    )
   583→                }
   584→
   585→                if (loggedAtDisplay == null && lat == null) {
   586→                    Text(
   587→                        text = "not yet logged",
   588→                        style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodySmall,
   589→                        color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant,
   590→                    )
   591→                }
   592→            }
   593→
   594→            HorizontalDivider()
   595→
   596→            // ==================================================================
   597→            // Save button
   598→            // ==================================================================
   599→
   600→            Button(
   601→                onClick = viewModel::onSaveTapped,
   602→                modifier = Modifier
   603→                    .fillMaxWidth()
   604→                    .padding(horizontal = 16.dp, vertical = 12.dp),
   605→                enabled = !state.isSaving,
   606→            ) {
   607→                Text("Save Changes")
   608→            }
   609→
   610→            Spacer(Modifier.height(16.dp))
   611→        }
   612→    }
   613→
   614→    // ==========================================================================
   615→    // Lens picker sheet
   616→    // ==========================================================================
   617→
   618→    if (showLensPicker) {
   619→        ModalBottomSheet(
   620→            onDismissRequest = { showLensPicker = false },
   621→            sheetState = rememberModalBottomSheetState(),
   622→        ) {
   623→            Text(
   624→                text = "Select Lens",
   625→                style = MaterialTheme.typography.titleMedium,
   626→                modifier = Modifier.padding(horizontal = 16.dp, vertical = 8.dp),
   627→            )
   628→            LazyColumn(modifier = Modifier.heightIn(max = 400.dp)) {
   629→                items(state.rollLenses, key = { it.id }) { lens ->
   630→                    LensPickerItem(
   631→                        lens = lens,
   632→                        isSelected = lens.id == state.selectedLensId,
   633→                        onClick = {
   634→                            viewModel.onLensSelected(lens.id)
   635→                            showLensPicker = false
   636→                        },
   637→                    )
   638→                }
   639→            }
   640→            Spacer(Modifier.height(16.dp))
   641→        }
   642→    }
   643→
   644→    // ==========================================================================
   645→    // Discard changes dialog
   646→    // ==========================================================================
   647→
   648→    if (showDiscardDialog) {
   649→        AlertDialog(
   650→            onDismissRequest = { showDiscardDialog = false },
   651→            title = { Text("Discard changes?") },
   652→            text = { Text("Your changes to this frame will be lost.") },
   653→            confirmButton = {
   654→                Button(
   655→                    onClick = {
   656→                        showDiscardDialog = false
   657→                        navController.popBackStack()
   658→                    },
   659→                ) {
   660→                    Text("Discard")
   661→                }
   662→            },
   663→            dismissButton = {
   664→                TextButton(onClick = { showDiscardDialog = false }) {
   665→                    Text("Keep editing")
   666→                }
   667→            },
   668→        )
   669→    }
   670→}
   671→
   672→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   673→// Large stepper (aperture + shutter speed)
   674→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   675→
   676→/**
   677→ * Big stepper control used for aperture and shutter speed.
   678→ * Mirrors the layout of the Quick Screen steppers: large tap targets, value centered.
   679→ *
   680→ * Button convention (consistent with camera dial direction):
   681→ *   Aperture:      + = wider (lower f-number), − = narrower (higher f-number)
   682→ *   Shutter speed: + = slower (more light),    − = faster  (less light)
   683→ */
   684→@Composable
   685→private fun LargeStepper(
   686→    value: String,
   687→    onDecrement: () -> Unit,
   688→    onIncrement: () -> Unit,
   689→    canDecrement: Boolean,
   690→    canIncrement: Boolean,
   691→    modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
   692→    valueColor: Color = Color.Unspecified,
   693→) {
   694→    Row(
   695→        modifier = modifier.fillMaxWidth(),
   696→        horizontalArrangement = Arrangement.Center,
   697→        verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically,
   698→    ) {
   699→        OutlinedButton(
   700→            onClick = onDecrement,
   701→            enabled = canDecrement,
   702→            modifier = Modifier.size(52.dp),
   703→            contentPadding = PaddingValues(0.dp),
   704→        ) {
   705→            Text(
   706→                text = "\u2212",
   707→                style = MaterialTheme.typography.headlineSmall,
   708→            )
   709→        }
   710→
   711→        Box(
   712→            modifier = Modifier
   713→                .padding(horizontal = 8.dp)
   714→                .width(108.dp),
   715→            contentAlignment = Alignment.Center,
   716→        ) {
   717→            Text(
   718→                text = value,
   719→                style = MaterialTheme.typography.titleLarge,
   720→                color = valueColor,
   721→                textAlign = TextAlign.Center,
   722→            )
   723→        }
   724→
   725→        OutlinedButton(
   726→            onClick = onIncrement,
   727→            enabled = canIncrement,
   728→            modifier = Modifier.size(52.dp),
   729→            contentPadding = PaddingValues(0.dp),
   730→        ) {
   731→            Text(
   732→                text = "+",
   733→                style = MaterialTheme.typography.headlineSmall,
   734→            )
   735→        }
   736→    }
   737→}
   738→
   739→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   740→// Lens picker list item
   741→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   742→
   743→@Composable
   744→private fun LensPickerItem(
   745→    lens: Lens,
   746→    isSelected: Boolean,
   747→    onClick: () -> Unit,
   748→) {
   749→    ListItem(
   750→        headlineContent = { Text(lens.name) },
   751→        supportingContent = { Text("${lens.make} \u00b7 f/${lens.maxAperture}") },
   752→        trailingContent = {
   753→            if (isSelected) {
   754→                Text(
   755→                    text = "current",
   756→                    style = MaterialTheme.typography.labelSmall,
   757→                    color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary,
   758→                )
   759→            }
   760→        },
   761→        modifier = Modifier.clickable(onClick = onClick),
   762→    )
   763→}
   764→

    84→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

    86→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    87→
    88→@OptIn(ExperimentalMaterial3Api::class, ExperimentalLayoutApi::class)
    89→@Composable
    90→fun QuickScreenScreen(navController: NavHostController) {
    91→    val viewModel: QuickScreenViewModel = viewModel()
    92→    val state by viewModel.state.collectAsStateWithLifecycle()
    93→    val context = LocalContext.current
    94→    val haptic = LocalHapticFeedback.current
    95→    val scope = rememberCoroutineScope()
    96→    val snackbarHostState = remember { SnackbarHostState() }
    97→
    98→    // GPS — FusedLocationProviderClient for frame log capture
    99→    val fusedLocationClient = remember { LocationServices.getFusedLocationProviderClient(context) }
   100→    var pendingLogAfterPermission by remember { mutableStateOf(false) }
   101→    val locationPermissionLauncher = rememberLauncherForActivityResult(
   102→        ActivityResultContracts.RequestPermission(),
   103→    ) { granted ->
   104→        if (pendingLogAfterPermission) {
   105→            pendingLogAfterPermission = false
   106→            if (granted) {
   107→                scope.launch { getLocationAndLog(fusedLocationClient, viewModel) }
   108→            } else {
   109→                // Permission denied — log without GPS rather than blocking the user
   110→                viewModel.onLogFrameTapped(null, null)
   111→            }
   112→        }
   113→    }
   114→
   115→    // Speech — dictate the note field
   116→    val speechLauncher = rememberLauncherForActivityResult(
   117→        ActivityResultContracts.StartActivityForResult(),
   118→    ) { result ->
   119→        val matches = result.data?.getStringArrayListExtra(RecognizerIntent.EXTRA_RESULTS)
   120→        matches?.firstOrNull()?.let { viewModel.onNoteChanged(it) }
   121→    }
   122→
   123→    // Dialog / sheet state
   124→    var showOverwriteDialog by remember { mutableStateOf(false) }
   125→    var overwriteFrameNumber by remember { mutableIntStateOf(0) }
   126→    var showRollCompleteDialog by remember { mutableStateOf(false) }
   127→    var showFilterPicker by remember { mutableStateOf(false) }
   128→
   129→    LaunchedEffect(Unit) {
   130→        viewModel.events.collect { event ->
   131→            when (event) {
   132→                is QuickScreenEvent.FrameLogged -> {
   133→                    haptic.performHapticFeedback(HapticFeedbackType.LongPress)
   134→                }
   135→                is QuickScreenEvent.ConfirmOverwrite -> {
   136→                    overwriteFrameNumber = event.frameNumber
   137→                    showOverwriteDialog = true
   138→                }
   139→                is QuickScreenEvent.RollComplete -> showRollCompleteDialog = true
   140→                is QuickScreenEvent.ShowErrorMessage -> {
   141→                    snackbarHostState.showSnackbar(event.message)
   142→                }
   143→            }
   144→        }
   145→    }
   146→
   147→    // Initiates Log Frame, requesting GPS location first if the roll requires it.
   148→    fun triggerLogFrame() {
   149→        val gpsEnabled = state.activeRoll?.roll?.gpsEnabled == true
   150→        if (!gpsEnabled) {
   151→            viewModel.onLogFrameTapped(null, null)
   152→            return
   153→        }
   154→        val permission = Manifest.permission.ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION
   155→        if (ContextCompat.checkSelfPermission(context, permission) == PackageManager.PERMISSION_GRANTED) {
   156→            scope.launch { getLocationAndLog(fusedLocationClient, viewModel) }
   157→        } else {
   158→            pendingLogAfterPermission = true
   159→            locationPermissionLauncher.launch(permission)
   160→        }
   161→    }
   162→
   163→    Scaffold(
   164→        snackbarHost = { SnackbarHost(snackbarHostState) },
   165→    ) { innerPadding ->
   166→        when {
   167→            state.isLoading -> {
   168→                Box(
   169→                    modifier = Modifier
   170→                        .fillMaxSize()
   171→                        .padding(innerPadding),
   172→                    contentAlignment = Alignment.Center,
   173→                ) {
   174→                    CircularProgressIndicator()
   175→                }
   176→            }
   177→
   178→            state.activeRoll == null -> {
   179→                EmptyState(
   180→                    modifier = Modifier
   181→                        .fillMaxSize()
   182→                        .padding(innerPadding),
   183→                    onCreateRoll = { navController.navigate(RollSetup) },
   184→                )
   185→            }
   186→
   187→            else -> {
   188→                // Safe: we just confirmed activeRoll is non-null above
   189→                val activeRoll = state.activeRoll!!
   190→                val rollLenses = activeRoll.lenses
   191→                val rollFilters = activeRoll.filters.map { it.filter }
   192→
   193→                // Compute visible filter chips: MRU-ordered, up to 4, then pad with remaining
   194→                val mruSet = state.mruFilterIds.toSet()
   195→                val displayedFilters = buildList {
   196→                    state.mruFilterIds.take(4)
   197→                        .mapNotNull { id -> rollFilters.firstOrNull { it.id == id } }
   198→                        .also { addAll(it) }
   199→                    if (size < 4) {
   200→                        rollFilters.filter { it.id !in mruSet }.take(4 - size).also { addAll(it) }
